items.title: As a 'clog wog', Australians didn't know I was a migrant, so they confided their racism
items.summary: Annette Jorgensen moved here from Denmark aged two. Living as a migrant while looking like a white Australian was an insightful experience.

items.title: South Australian mother faces deportation after passport mistake

items.title: If your stomach feels more sensitive than usual, this could be the reason

items.summary: The Iraq War left deep scars for both Iraqi civilians and former US soldiers still coming to terms with their actions.

items.title: Why the 'flow-on effects' from re-opening the Strait of Hormuz could take months

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith to remain in custody while charged with war crimes
items.summary: Australia's most decorated war veteran, Ben Roberts-Smith will remain in custody after a brief court mention this morning. The Victoria Cross recipient has been charged with five counts of the war crime of murder. Phoebe Deas reports from Silverwater correctional complex in Sydney.

items.summary: As traditional views of masculinity regain traction among some young men, Kelvin is challenging ideas around a father's role.

items.title: Trump announces 'double-sided' ceasefire as Iran gives two-week Hormuz passage pledge

items.summary: The US president's inconsistent messaging about the Strait of Hormuz signals no "clear strategy", experts say.

items.summary: Iran has rejected Trump's demands and threatened to retaliate against infrastructure belonging to US allies in the Gulf.

items.title: Human Rights Commission accepts racial discrimination complaint against NSW minister
